name: WP-E15-catch-hygiene: log/escalate silent-failure catch blocks + webhook error context
description: Sweep the silent-failure catch-block surface. Repo rule context: silent recovery without escalation is BANNED in billing, auth, and webhook code — emit a structured metric or Inngest event via safeSend(); console.warn alone is not enough (AGENTS.md Fix Development Rules). Findings: F-022 (silent-failure catch blocks across billing/session/family — bare catch, no log/escalation) · F-047 (silent swallow of DB failure fetching dictation struggles — bare catch {}) · F-048 (consent resend-counter rollback swallowed without logging — GDPR-adjacent) · F-049 (signature-verification catch discards error detail on Stripe/Resend webhook routes). Full finding text: docs/audit/2026-05-29-full-audit/L-gap-delta.md (label errors-api).
acceptanceCriteria: No bare catch {} remains in the swept surfaces; every fix emits a structured signal (safeSend()/Sentry per repo conventions, with core-send comments where dispatch failure must short-circuit); webhook signature-verification failures carry error detail; regression test per finding.
riskImpact: P1: standing repo-rule violations (silent-recovery ban in billing/auth/webhook code). Failures in billing, consent (GDPR-adjacent), and webhook verification currently vanish without signal — operational blindness on revenue- and compliance-critical paths. Fix risk is low (signal emission only, no behavior change), but webhook surfaces (Stripe/Resend) need care not to leak secrets into logs.
bodyText:
Bundle brief — WP-E15-catch-hygiene (WP DoR bridge, PRG-15)
Rationale: silent recovery without escalation is BANNED in billing, auth, and webhook code (repo AGENTS.md, Fix Development Rules) — the four findings below are standing rule-violations, not hygiene, hence P1. Bundled because all four share one surface class: catch-block discipline (three silence findings + one missing-context finding on the same kind of block).
Strategy: one targeted sweep PR. For each finding, classify the raw error, then either escalate via a structured signal — safeSend() for non-core dispatches (Sentry-captured, never throws), bare inngest.send only for CORE flows with a core-send comment — or log with full context. console.warn alone is insufficient. Classify errors before formatting (Code Quality Guards). No behavior change beyond signal emission; user-facing flow must be unchanged.
Scope — findings absorbed (full text: docs/audit/2026-05-29-full-audit/L-gap-delta.md, label errors-api)
F-022 — Silent-failure catch blocks across billing/session/family: bare catch or empty return with no log/escalation (audit refs architecture-audit-F3a…F3e).
F-047 — Silent swallow of DB failure when fetching dictation struggles: bare catch {} with no logging or Sentry (errors-api-1).
F-048 — Consent resend-counter rollback failure swallowed without logging, GDPR-adjacent; inconsistent with two sibling catch blocks (errors-api-2).
F-049 — Signature-verification catch discards underlying error detail: Stripe and Resend webhook routes log no context on verification failure (errors-api-3).
Bundle Acceptance Criteria
No bare catch {} / silent empty-return catch remains in the swept surfaces (billing, session, family, dictation struggles, consent rollback).
Every silent-failure fix emits a structured signal per repo conventions: safeSend() Inngest event or Sentry capture; core-send comment where dispatch failure must short-circuit. console.warn alone is not acceptable.
Stripe and Resend webhook signature-verification failures log the underlying error detail (without leaking secrets/PII).
Regression coverage per finding: a test that fails without the fix and passes with it (red-green evidence), per finding cluster.
3+ sibling drift rule honored: if a fixed pattern has 3+ sibling sites, install a forward-only guard test or document a tracked deferred sweep in the same PR.
Provenance: 2 thin children carry absorbed provenance; lifecycle rides this parent — children are never claimed/executed standalone (ZDX-ADR-0001 pattern, IF bridge ruling 2026-06-10 extended to PRG-15).
